channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's new energy order puts states' climate laws in the crosshairs of the Department of Justice
item.description: A new executive order from President Donald Trump that's part of his effort to invigorate energy production launches the possibility that his Department of Justice will go to court against state laws aimed at slashing planet-warming greenhouse gas pollution from fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House passes bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in a move that would vastly diminish the ability of courts to block President Trump’s policies. Dubbed the No Rogue Rulings Act, the legislation from Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Republican-led House passes bill to limit nationwide orders from federal district judges
item.description: The House has passed a bill that limits the authority of federal district judges to issue nationwide orders.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump-backed bill to stop 'rogue' judges passes House
item.description: House lawmakers advanced a bill to block district judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in most cases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump lets the water flow
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has long complained about modern rules that limit water flow for showerheads, making it harder for him to wash his "beautiful hair.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Options for Trump's space-based 'Golden Dome' missile defense shield head to Hegseth for approval
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — U.S. Space Command said Wednesday that it has finalized options for President Donald Trump's “Golden Dome” missile defense system and has sent its recommendations to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for his review and approval.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump orders probe into 'Anonymous' New York Times editorial writer
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed a pair of memorandums directing the Department of Justice to investigate two individuals who served in his first administration and pushed back against Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court Chief Justice Roberts swoops in to save Trump firing decision
item.description: Supreme Court Chief Justice John Roberts intervened to preserve the Trump administration's termination of two federal board members, a temporary win for the administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arvin approves special election for vacant city council seat
item.description: ARVIN, Calif. (KGET) -- The City of Arvin approved a special election to fill a vacant seat on the city council. The fully vote-by-mail election is scheduled for August. The decision was met with cheers from the public in attendance at Tuesday's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Treviño, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota rolls out dashboard tracking federal funding cuts in the state
item.description: As of Wednesday, the site reported roughly $296 million in federal grants to Minnesota had been cut and millions more expected to be cancelled.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to lead ATF, replacing FBI Director Kash Patel
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel was replaced by U.S. Army Secretary Dan Driscoll to serve as the acting director of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to host El Salvador's president amid US deportation efforts
item.description: President Trump will welcome the leader of El Salvador at the White House next week to tout cooperation between the two nations as the U.S. sends deported migrants they claim are gang members to a massive El Salvadoran prison.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed off his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump officials halt $1B in funding for Cornell, $790M for Northwestern
item.description: The move is part of a broader push to use government funding to get major academic institutions to comply with President Donald Trump ’s political agenda.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Companies need 'time machine' for tariffs to make sense: Andrew Yang
item.description: "Instead of reshoring manufacturing, [CEOs] are just cutting investment, they're laying people off," Yang said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Biden believes he would've beaten Trump in election: Author
item.description: Author Chris Whipple released "Uncharted: How Trump Beat Biden, Harris, and the Odds in the Wildest Campaign in History" on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House GOP leaders prep vote on budget despite rift within the party
item.description: Republicans in Congress are closer to passing key elements of President Trump's legislative agenda — like extending tax cuts that expire at the end of the year — but only if the House and Senate can get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Lombardo's crime bill focuses on harsher penalties in Nevada, penalizing repeat offenders
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is proposing harsher penalties for repeat offenders, DUI drivers and other criminals as part of his sweeping crime bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration: U of M student in ICE custody should remain jailed
item.description: The Trump administration says a University of Minnesota graduate student from Turkey should remain jailed while the Department of Homeland Security moves forward with removal proceedings. That's according to court documents filed just ahead of a deadline late Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: U.S. stocks are volatile as selloffs sweep through global markets
item.description: Shares nosedived around the world Monday as higher U.S. tariffs and a backlash from Beijing triggered massive sell-offs. Trump has defended the tariffs: “You have to take medicine to fix something.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman and John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Rep. slams 'government-funded kidnapping program' after UF student is detained by ICE
item.description: A Florida Representative is calling out President Donald Trump and his administration for running a "government-funded kidnapping program" following ICE's recent detainment of a University of Florida student.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Conservative website‘s bid to access Sen. Mitchell body camera footage revived by appeals court
item.description: Alpha News sought to compel law enforcement to release certain video related to Sen. Nicole Mitchell’s arrest in April 2024. An appeals court ruling has reopened the possibility of body and dash camera footage being released ahead of her trial.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrat introduces proposal to ban book bans
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would ban school districts and staff from banning books without a court order determining the material is obscene.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sen. Champion steps down as ethics committee chair amid conflict-of-interest questions
item.description: Minnesota Senate President Bobby Joe Champion will step down as head of the Subcommittee on Ethics as he faces questions about a possible conflict of interest related to his legal work and funding bills he has sponsored.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
